events.name: Oakland First Fridays
events.description: Monthly art walk & street fair. First Friday of every month. Rolling applications.
events.scheduleNote: Monthly art walk & street fair. First Friday of every month. Rolling applications.

opportunity.fee: Dessert vendors $250-$400; food trucks $300-$600; food booths $450-$700
opportunity.eligibility: Food vendors need California seller, Oakland business, Alameda County health, insurance, and applicable fire permits.

recurrence.summary: First Friday of each month; check the official schedule for exceptions.

events.name: Foodwise Ferry Plaza Farmers Market
events.description: Year-round Foodwise farmers market with prepared-food sellers at the San Francisco Ferry Building.
events.scheduleNote: Weekly Saturday market (Tues & Thurs too). Premium location. Competitive entry.

opportunity.eligibility: Prepared-food sellers are considered; Foodwise prefers organic, regional ingredients and at least two years of relevant experience. Products undergo review and tasting.

recurrence.summary: Tuesday, Thursday, and Saturday year-round; check official updates for holiday exceptions.

events.name: Castro Street Fair
events.description: 52nd Annual. SF legacy street fair celebrating LGBTQ+ community. Thousands of attendees. Food vendors support local nonprofits. Great community event.

opportunity.eligibility: Exhibitor registration is open; confirm the food-vendor category and requirements in the official application.

events.name: PCFMA Bay Area Farmers Markets
events.description: Rolling interest program for Pacific Coast Farmers’ Market Association markets. Individual market placement depends on organizer review and availability.
events.scheduleNote: Choose preferred markets during organizer screening.

opportunity.eligibility: Food producers may submit the official interest form; individual market availability is confirmed by PCFMA.

events.name: AIM Farmers Markets Vendor Program
events.description: Agricultural Institute of Marin reviews prepared-food, TFF, onsite-consumption, and food-truck applicants for its market network.
events.scheduleNote: Applications are reviewed within 45 days; placement is market-specific.

opportunity.eligibility: Prepared food must be producer-made and is subject to organizer review and tasting.

events.name: Urban Village Farmers’ Markets Vendor Program
events.description: Official application route for prepared and hot food or drinks sold for immediate onsite consumption.
events.scheduleNote: Accepted vendors are generally expected to attend weekly.

opportunity.eligibility: Requires commissary production, permits, insurance, food/fire compliance, and organizer approval.

events.name: Downtown First Thursdays Street Party
events.description: Monthly downtown street party with an official call for San Francisco food and retail vendors.

recurrence.summary: First Thursday monthly through December 2026, 5–10 PM.

opportunity.eligibility: Food and retail celebrating San Francisco; the login-gated portal does not publicly prove application availability.

events.name: Bhangra & Beats Night Market
events.description: Night market celebrating Indian and South Asian cultures with an official food and retail vendor call.

opportunity.eligibility: Food offering should fit the event’s Indian and South Asian cultural focus; portal availability is not public.

events.name: Haight Ashbury Street Fair
events.description: Long-running neighborhood fair with explicit 2026 food-vendor applications and permit resources.

opportunity.eligibility: Non-flame food vending is explicitly supported; permits, insurance, and organizer approval are required.

events.name: Renegade Craft SF Winter
events.description: Curated holiday fair that explicitly welcomes food and drink participants and supports dedicated power arrangements for beverage service.

opportunity.fee: $800 full booth; food truck 15% gross with $200 minimum
opportunity.eligibility: Juried creative-business event; food/beverage vendors must coordinate equipment and power requirements.

events.name: Sunset Mercantile Vendor Program
events.description: Organizer network for recurring farmers markets, neighborhood markets, night markets, and seasonal fairs across San Francisco.
events.scheduleNote: Event dates and availability are selected inside the organizer's application portals.

opportunity.fee: Event-specific application and booth fees; food permits and fire requirements vary by event.
opportunity.eligibility: Food vendors, coffee and drink businesses, cottage food operators, and food trucks are supported at selected events.

events.name: Fellowship of Craft Winter 2026
events.location: Henry J. Kaiser Center for the Arts, Oakland
events.description: Juried holiday craft event with an explicit prepared-food exhibitor category.

opportunity.fee: Prepared-food permit approximately $211; exhibitor booth pricing is application-specific.
opportunity.eligibility: Artists, craftspeople, packaged-food businesses, and prepared-food exhibitors are eligible under the organizer's stated rules.

events.name: San José Made Vendor Opportunities
events.description: Official organizer calendar for South Bay fairs, night markets, holiday markets, and vendor applications.
events.scheduleNote: Applications are event-specific; only opportunities linked from the official organizer site should be trusted.

opportunity.fee: Varies by event and vendor type.
opportunity.eligibility: Food eligibility varies: some events accept packaged food and drinks while cooking vendors may be routed to Moveable Feast.

events.name: Laurel StreetFair World Music Festival
events.description: Large Oakland neighborhood festival with explicit packaged food, hot food, cart, and food-truck vendor categories.

opportunity.fee: Packaged food $375; hot 10x20 $550; cart $300; food truck $750–$800, plus processing and deposit requirements.
opportunity.eligibility: Food vendors must meet Alameda County permit, insurance, payment, and organizer approval requirements.

events.name: Off the Grid 2026 Market Applications
events.description: Official application hub for food creators seeking placement at Off the Grid's 2026 Bay Area markets.
events.scheduleNote: Market selection and operating dates are listed in the official application experience.

opportunity.eligibility: Food trucks, food stalls, beverage businesses, and other food creators are routed through Off the Grid's creator application.

events.name: San Francisco Coffee Festival
events.description: Specialty coffee festival featuring roasters, coffee shops, exhibitors, and food; no public 2026 vendor application status is stated.

opportunity.eligibility: The event features roasters, coffee shops, exhibitors, and food, but no public current-year application route is stated.

events.name: Solano Avenue Stroll
events.description: Large East Bay street festival with current registration for prepared and prepackaged food vendors.

opportunity.fee: $590 prepackaged food; $960 food prepared onsite, plus safety fee
opportunity.eligibility: Food vendors must provide permits, insurance when required, and seller documentation.

events.name: Presidio Pop Up Fall/Winter
events.description: Six-month mobile-food program accepting trucks, trailers, carts, and tents.
events.scheduleNote: November 1, 2026–March 31, 2027; minimum two shifts weekly if selected.

opportunity.fee: Tiered daily fee by asset and weekday/weekend
opportunity.eligibility: Bay Area mobile food businesses using trucks, trailers, carts, or tents.

events.name: San Francisco VegFest
events.description: Plant-based festival with an explicit food court and espresso-bar eligibility.

opportunity.fee: $225 food-court booth plus applicable SF health fees
opportunity.eligibility: All products must be 100% vegan; espresso bars and food vendors are explicitly supported.

events.name: South San Francisco Concert in the Park
events.location: Orange Memorial Park, South San Francisco
events.description: City-produced concert accepting direct food-vendor inquiries.
events.scheduleNote: 2026 event; official page controls the current date and selection timeline.

opportunity.eligibility: Food vendors must meet city business-license, county health, and insurance requirements.

events.name: Napa Farmers Market Vendor Program
events.description: Year-round Saturday market and seasonal Tuesday market with prospective food-vendor intake.

opportunity.eligibility: New food and artisan vendors may apply to the Saturday waitlist; placement is not guaranteed.

events.name: 365 Night Market
events.description: Monthly cultural night-market series with food, beverage, food-truck, and product-vendor intake.
events.scheduleNote: Monthly through October 2026; consult the official upcoming-events page for individual dates.

opportunity.fee: Varies by vendor type and market date
opportunity.eligibility: Food and beverage vendors require applicable Santa Clara County health documentation and insurance.

events.name: Bizerkeley Food Fest
events.description: Plant-based food festival with dedicated food and food-truck applications.

opportunity.eligibility: Food must be dairy- and meat-free; food trucks and established/new food businesses may apply.

events.name: Sonoma Tuesday Night Market
events.description: City-supported weekly market accepting packaged and prepared-food vendors.

opportunity.eligibility: Prepared food, packaged food, agricultural, and artisan vendors may apply.

events.name: Heart of the City Farmers Market Vendor Program
events.description: Ongoing San Francisco market intake for hot food, packaged food, cottage food, and permitted food trucks.
events.scheduleNote: Market participation is assigned by the organizer.

opportunity.eligibility: Hot-food, packaged-food, cottage-food, farm, and permitted food-truck categories are supported.

source.publisher: Heart of the City Farmers Market

events.name: San Francisco Greek Food Festival
events.description: Annual Greek food festival with an official current-year vendor application.

opportunity.eligibility: Vendor participation is subject to organizer approval and SF permit requirements.
